characters.name: A106
characters.description: A106Other NamesSixGenderMaleRaceRobotResidenceNerima University, Laboratory 7Horsepower1,000 A106or6is the main protagonist robot ofAtom: The Beginning. He was developed byHiroshi OchanomizuandUmataro Tenma. He is the prototype to Astro. He was created by Umataro and Hiroshi to show off their new system.

characters.name: Acheron
characters.description: AcheronJapanese Nameダーク・プルートウEnglish NameAcheronOther NamesDark Pluto (Japanese version)GenderMaleRaceRobotFamilyShadow (creator)Horsepower1,000,000Japanese VAAkio OotsukaEnglish VADavid Rasner  Acheronis an exact duplicate ofPlutocreated byShadow. Unlike Pluto, Acheron was built withoutKokoroprogramming. He is much more dangerous and very hard to control. He appears exclusively in the2003 Astro Boy anime series, filling the role normally filled byBorain theGreatest Robot in the Worldarc. Acheron behaves like a much colder Pluto. While he has the same voice and base characteristics, he has no developed emotions, due to his lack of kokoro programming. He simply follows basic orders that have been pre-programmed into his system

characters.name: Anton
characters.description: AntonJapanese NameアントンGenderMaleRaceHumanOccupationUniversity studentJapanese VAKappei YamaguchiEnglish VAWally Wingert Antonis an engineering student in theAstro Boy 2003 series. He is a skittish but good-natured young inventor with a crush onBrianna. In spite of an indirect romantic rivalry, he quickly becomes an ally and friend to Astro. Anton is a young human man around the age of 19-22. He has a tan complexion, and short red hair with curtained bangs and a very slight mullet down his neck. He is rather short, and gangly in stature. He usually wears loose-fitting casual clothes or his university's uniform. Anton is naturally a very nervous person, but loves technology and is eager to become a professional inventor. Despite his big aspirations, some of his devices are prone to malfunctions or adverse effects. Brianna describes him as having lots of potential but says he needs to focus better. Anton has a huge crush on Brianna, to a point where he loses sleep over it. He enlists Astro's help in writing a love letter under his name, taking a call with his simulated voice, and getting suggestions from Astro on what to say. However, his inability to tell Brianna directly leads to her feeling cheated when she finds out the truth. In an attempt to impress Brianna before she goes on a space mission, he rewires part of the rocket's system to project congratulatory flowers and dancing chibi versions of himself. This unfortunately triggers the rocket's auto-pilot and disables the guidance system. Astro is able to chip in help in time, and Anton comes out of it with a hope to further refine his inventions.

characters.name: Astro Boy (character)
characters.description: This article is for the character. For other uses, seehere. Astro BoyJapanese NameアトムEnglish NameAstroOther NamesAtomMighty AtomIron Arm AtomTobyThe Angel of VietnamTobioGenderMaleRaceRobotFamilyDr. Ochanomizu(adopted father)Momo(pet robot / white ostrich)Robita(robot)Uran(sister)YukoUmataro Tenma(creator/adopted father)Cobalt(brother)Jump(dog)Chiitan(brother)BirthdayApril 7, 2003[1]AffiliationDr. Ochanomizu(guardian)Dr. Tenma(Creator)OccupationHeroMinistry Of Science AssistantResidenceMinistry Of ScienceRobot Circus (former)Horsepower100,000Japanese VAMari Shimizu (1963 and 1980 series)Makoto Tsumura (2003 series)Aya Ueto (2009 film)Yoko Hikasa (Pluto)English VABillie Lou Watt (1963 dub)Patricia Kugler Whitely (1980 dub)Candi Milo (2003 dub)Freddie Highmore (2009 film)Kevin Rodriguez (Go Astroboy Go dub)Laura Stahl (Pluto dub)Rivals/EnemiesAtlasDr. PavlosRockPlutoAllies/FriendsAtlas(Sometimes)Blue KnightEpsilonEmilyHerculesUranDelta  Astro was created in the image of Tobio Tenma therefore he inherited his basic personality traits. Due to Tobio being a child when he died, Astro has inherited his same childlike innocence including his childlike naivety, Astro by nature has a strong sense of justice and for deciding what is right and wrong. Mostly a pacifist, Astro will not stray from using force if he has no choice, although he always tries to solve situations diplomatically. Astro is incredibly protective of family and friends and will often risk his own life to protect them from harm. Astro is also very sensitive, and tends to get hurt easily. Astro is selfless, brave and kindhearted. He has a deep love for humanity, in spite of the many hardships he may face. At the end of both the 1960s anime series andAstro Boy: Omega Factor, he willingly sacrificed himself in order to save humanity from certain doom (although in the latter, he was brought back to life). Astro's most complicated relationship is with his own creator Umataro Tenma. Although, he was treated with love and care by Tenma in the beginning, the grief left over after the death of his son Tobio took control causing Tenma to accept that a robot could not be an adequate replacement for his flesh and blood human child. Due to this realization, Tenma slowly begins to lose his sanity and in some adaptations resorts to drinking, he begins to treat Astro poorly and even becomes outright hostile towards him leaving him stuck in dangerous situations. In most adaptations Astro is eventually sold to Hamegg's Robot Circus by Tenma and although Tenma feels legitimate remorse for his actions, fails to reconnect with Astro who mostly tries to avoid him however, in some instances tries to forgive him which results in Ochanomizu typically going out of his way to stop such a reunion.

characters.name: Astro II
characters.description: Astro IIOther NamesAtom IIGenderMaleRaceRobot Astro IIis an experimental second version ofAstro Boy. While he was programmed with the intent of making him more "human", he turns out to be a lecherous layabout. He appears only once in the series, in a oneshot manga chapter in volume 23. Astro II was built by order of the Japanese government after Astro's self-sacrifice to save the Earth (as shown in the final episode ofAstro Boy, which came out not long before this chapter did). Astro II was built in hopes of creating a more human robot, and a number of experimental procedures went into his production, one of which was the choice to give him humanoid genitalia. This turned out to be disastrous, as Astro II is shown to be greedy, perverted, and with little interest in helping others. Eventually, Astro II was arrested for impersonating Astro Boy, after the government had enough of his antics.

characters.name: Astro's parents
characters.description: Astro's robot parentsJapanese Nameリン & エタノール, ママとパパOther NamesRebecca & Elmer Dobbs (NOW! Comics series)GenderFemale (Rin), male (Ethanol)RaceRobotFamilyAstro,Uran,CobaltandChi-Tan(adopted children) (1963 anime only)StatusAliveHorsepower500hp eachEnglish VABecky Wilenski and Jay Rath (1980 dub) Astro's parentsare two robots made byProfessor Ochanomizuto be Astro's parental units. They are designed to act and look like normal humans. They are usually unnamed, but Japanese reference books refer to Astro's mother as Rin (the Japanese word for Phosphorus), and Astro's father as Ethanol[1]. Astro's parents primarily appear inOsamu Tezuka's manga, and the 1963 anime series. They live in a modern house like humans, and look afterAstro,Uran, andCobaltand Chiitan if applicable. In some stories, it is evident that they are younger than Astro in construction, and sometimes find that they have to learn from him. Astro's parents usually call each other, "dear" instead of names.

characters.name: Atlas/2003
characters.description: AtlasAge18GenderMaleRaceRobotHuman (former)OccupationGang Leader (formerly) Human (former) Atlas is a robot in the2003 animeversion of Astro Boy. He's a robot withkokoro, a program that gives robots freewill and emotions and was formerly human. He serves as a temporary villain and eventually helpsAstrostop the fighting between humans and robots.

characters.name: Atom Cat
characters.description: Atom Catis a manga series written byOsamu Tezuka.  The series was published inNico Nico Comicsfrom July 1986 to February 1987[1]. The series was intended as a return to theAstro Boycharacter, however it includes a completely new cast of characters and was reworked into a comical theme.

characters.name: Atom Cat

The series was intended as a return to theAstro Boycharacter, however it includes a completely new cast of characters and was reworked into a comical theme.

characters.name: Atom Cat (Character)
characters.description: Tsugio meeting the newly built Atom! Atom Catwas a tomcat that was found in a box by a young boy namedTsugio. After taking him home, and his parents allowing him to keep it if he took care of it, he named him "Atom" (Astro in the translation) after his favorite manga hero,Tetsuwan Atom (Astro Boy). But while driving his bicycle, Tsugio crashed into a car, which happened to be piloted by alien visitors. The alien visitors "rebuild" the cat, saying that it'd be a shame if the cat died and the boy started crying. They confuse the tomcat named Atom for the manga character, and rebuilt him as such! Atom Cat now has all of Astro Boys powers! Including flight, and super-strength, which Atom shows off the most.

characters.name: Bacon Egg
characters.description: Bacon EggJapanese Nameベーコン・エッグGenderMaleRaceHumanFamilyHamegg(son) Bacon Eggis the host of the World Robot Battling arena in Australia, inAtom: The Beginning. He is tremendously theatric and a natural hypeman. Given his similar name (i.e. pork product + "egg") and appearance, Bacon Egg also appears to beHamegg's father. In the arena, Bacon has a bold and captivating stage persona. He is able to keep up with the moment-by-moment action of the robot tournaments, giving his audience constant commentary. His thoughts on AI robots are mixed. He has empathy for some of the more humanized robots, such as U-Ran, but enjoys general robot violence. Bacon sees the carnage as something that's necessary to entertain an audience. However, after being rescued by North, Bacon seems to have a very slight change of heart.

characters.name: Belligerent
characters.description: BelligerentJapanese NameベリゲラントGenderGenderless (Male programming)RaceRobotAffiliationHamegg(creator)OccupationBattlebotStatusDeceased  Belligerentis one of the battle robots that take part inHamegg's Robot Games. He exclusively appears as a minor antagonist in the2009 Astro Boy film. He merges withOutrageousto form The Little Stinker.

characters.name: Bem
characters.description: BemJapanese NameベムEnglish NameBeamoOther NamesDolly (Jetter Mars)Cosmo Boy (Go Astro Boy Go!)GenderN/AFemale (Jetter Mars)Male (Go Astro Boy Go!)RaceAlien robotOccupationBombJapanese VAMasako Sugaya (1963 series)Samiko Tamigawa (2001 film)English VABillie Lou Watt (1963 series) Cosmo Boy (Go Astro Boy Go!) Female (Jetter Mars) As a newly-made robot, Bem is naive, and they can be stubborn and emotional. However, they are benevolent and kind by nature. Bem was especially horrified to learn about their hidden power, which caused them to flee Nicoro in hopes of avoiding an explosive death. Unfortunately, Bem does not fully understand how their powers work yet. Bem is also very fond of Astro. Since Astro was the first person to treat Bem as an equal and not a weapon, they come to trust him almost immediately. When Astro attempts to contain Bem, they do not hesitate to fight back, but soon regret hurting their first friend.

Female (Jetter Mars) As a newly-made robot, Bem is naive, and they can be stubborn and emotional. However, they are benevolent and kind by nature. Bem was especially horrified to learn about their hidden power, which caused them to flee Nicoro in hopes of avoiding an explosive death. Unfortunately, Bem does not fully understand how their powers work yet. Bem is also very fond of Astro. Since Astro was the first person to treat Bem as an equal and not a weapon, they come to trust him almost immediately. When Astro attempts to contain Bem, they do not hesitate to fight back, but soon regret hurting their first friend.

characters.name: Big Titan
characters.description: In the 1963 series Big Titan is built by an Indian man named Doctor Buck, who wants revenge for losing his daughter in a missile strike, and revenge on Astro Boy for destroying his Robot Chief, who kidnappedAstro Girl, who Doctor Buck mistakes for his long-lost daughter. Big Titan highly resembles Pluto, even having 1 million horsepower, though he isn't. Big Titan also appears on the cover of the second Ultra Box Set of Astro Boy DVDs.

characters.name: Big Titan
characters.description: In the 1963 series Big Titan is built by an Indian man named Doctor Buck, who wants revenge for losing his daughter in a missile strike, and revenge on Astro Boy for destroying his Robot Chief, who kidnappedAstro Girl, who Doctor Buck mistakes for his long-lost daughter. Big Titan highly resembles Pluto, even having 1 million horsepower, though he isn't. Big Titan also appears on the cover of the second Ultra Box Set of Astro Boy DVDs.

characters.name: Black Jack
characters.description: Black JackJapanese Nameブラック・ジャック間黒男English NameDr. Rojet (1980 English dub)Dr. Swenson (1985 Canadian dub)Other NamesKuroo Hazama (birth name)GenderMaleRaceHumanFamilyMother (deceased)Father & step-motherPinoko (adoptive daughter)OccupationMedical doctor (unlicensed)ResidenceJapanJapanese VANachi Nozawa (1980 Astro Boy anime, Marine Express)Masato Ibu (Phoenix 2772)Akio Ootsuka (1993-present)English VADel Lewis (1980 Astro Boy anime)Kirk Thorton (1993-present) 間黒男 Dr. Swenson (1985 Canadian dub) Black Jack is smug but charismatic. He is as stubborn as he is gifted, which is exactly what cost him his medical license: when he was in medical school, Black Jack's classmate and lover Kei Kisaragi (formerly Megumi) was diagnosed with late stage ovarian cancer. Their superiors claimed Kisaragi's cancer was too far gone to operate on, but Black Jack, confident that he could save Kisaragi, defied their orders and operated on him anyway. Kisaragi's life was saved, but Black Jack's medical license was immediately revoked. Regardless, he continued to hone his craft as a doctor. Black Jack's outward ego and image are a cover-up for years of childhood trauma. Born as Kuroo Hazama, he was a normal child until he and his mother Mio went to a beach, where they stepped on an abandoned, undetonated IED land mine. Mio was killed while Kuroo needed intense full-body surgery, and instead of supporting the family, Kuroo's father Kagemitsu instead chose to abandon them and remarry. Throughout the series, Black Jack tries to deal with his resentment towards his father and step family. The only person who can get under Black Jack's skin almost as badly as his father isDr. Kiriko. The two have starkly opposing views on life and death, and have competed to save vs. euthanize a patient. Elsewhere, Black Jack is fiercely loyal to those he lets close to him, such as Pinoko and his mentorDr. Honma. Honma was also the one who reconstructed and rehabilitated Black Jack after his childhood accident.

characters.name: Black Lux
characters.description: This article is for the character "Black Lux”. For other uses, seeBlack Lux (disambiguation) Black Lux is a gangster who robs and kills robots, stealing their money and components.  He's driven by a desire to avenge his mother, who he believes was killed by robots. The story begins when a mysterious package containing robot parts is sent tp Dr. Elephun.  He puts the pieces  back together  to form a headless robot body which Astro takes to Mt. Aurora in Antartica.  There, Mr. Mustache attacks a bus driver for refusing to take on Astro as a passenger  and is sent to prison   Astro goes to Mt Aurora, where Lux and his men attack the heavy robots running a whale farm on Mt. Aurora.  After the robots collapse the criling of their home on the Lux gang, they call for medical help for the guys, who are cared for by robot nurses and feel badly about what they've done.  After learning of Lux's tragic past from a cellmate, Mr. Mustache escapes but is capturee by Lux and taken to his hideout in Capetown, Africa.  Astro also goes there and meets the mystery robot's creator.  From him, Astro learns that her name is Aurora and that she was raising a baby who she named Lux as her son.  When Aurora's rebuilt, she begins to worry about Lux.  Meanwhile, Astro defeats the Lux gang and captures Lux himself, unmasking him as a young boy - the one orphaned allegedly by robots.  He and Aurora are reunited and he falls into her lap, overcome by joy.

characters.name: Blackie Young
characters.description: Blackie Young is a Junior High School gangster leader that hates robots. He and his men always wear black masks and black suits. They destroy and steal from robots, but never from women, mainly because Blackie's mother was killed by robots and he has a personal vendetta against them all. In the 1980s episode Blackie Young, Astro finds out that Blackie's mother is actually a nurse robot named Aurora (Rosaline in Japanese). Aurora wanted to adopt Blackie when he was little but the robot laws don't allow it. When she went to a robot rights protest, she was destroyed. Blackie reconciles his mistakes and Aurora takes him back with the unconditional love only a mother can provide to her son.

characters.name: Blue Knight
characters.description: The Blue KnightJapanese Name青騎士 (Aokishi)English NameThe Blue KnightOther NamesBlue BonGenderMaleRaceRobotFamilyMaria(younger sister)Tonto(younger brother)OccupationWarriorJapanese VAYoshio Kaneuchi (1963 series)Hideyuki Tanaka (2003 series)English VAWally WingertRivals/EnemiesAstro Boy (character)UranDeltaAllies/FriendsAstro Boy (character)(sometimes)AtlasEpsilonEmilyHerculesPluto  TheBlue Knight(akaBlue Bon) is a renegade super-powered robot who crusades for robot rights. He is bold and noble like a medieval knight, but secretly holds a tragic past. UnlikeAstro Boy, the Blue Knight despises humans, and sees them as a threat to robots everywhere. Blue Knight is prejudiced towards all humans due to his past experience with them, refusing to communicate or listen to anyone and is hostile towards those that have harmed other robots. He also feels no remorse if humans are killed in his attacks nor does he hesitate to destroy other robots who oppose him including. Despite his views, he is not heartless, and was willing to work with Astro Boy to save humans on multiple occasions, though usually only if fellow robots were in peril as well.

characters.name: Blues
characters.description: BluesJapanese NameブルースGenderMaleRaceHumanAffiliationSkunk Kusai's gangOccupationGang memberJapanese VAKouji YusaEnglish VABill Farmer  Bluesis one ofSkunk Kusai's two henchmen in the 2003 series. He is a large, bulky man who is usually very quiet and shy. His specialty with the gang is acting as backup muscle and driving their getaway vehicles. Blues works under Skunk, and alongside Jazz as a partner. He can most often be seen working alongside Jazz, and the two communicate like good friends and equals. Blues is the largest member of Skunk's gang, and also the one who speaks the least. He only adds remarks alongside his cohorts or asks questions when he finds it necessary. He is the most passive member of the group, usually letting Skunk or Jazz take the lead in schemes and following along to help. InJumbo Revived, he is visibly nervous about piloting Jumbo against the heavily-armed police, hinting that he is more used to low key underground crime. He toleratedDenkou, but was openly irritated upon finding out they had talked to Astro. His main role among Skunk's group is as backup muscle, occasionally working with computers and driving their vehicles. InOld Dog, New Tricks, his pockets are emptied by a super magnet, and it is revealed that Blues carries a pocket knife for close range self-defense.

characters.name: Bora
characters.description: Bora in the Manga Bora in the 1980 version of Astro Boy. Bora is a Robot from the 2nd part of "The Greatest Robot on Earth" and has 2-million horsepower. He and Pluto were both made by the same person, and he possessed the power to cause damage by screaming his own name. After he fights Pluto, he is destroyed by Astro boy from the inside. He was originally a rotundish giant robot, but in the 1980s anime, he is re-imagined as a horrible monstrosity of a robot with 2 bird-heads, clawed feet, long arms, a tail, and no torso.

characters.name: Bora

characters.name: Brando
characters.description: Brando in the original manga. Brandois a Turkish wrestling robot who appears inThe Greatest Robot in the World. He is a big burly robot that has the ability to spin at high velocity and turn into a deadly sphere that grinds up anything it comes in contact with. Brando fightsPlutoto avenge the death of his friend,Montblanc; despite this, he tells Astro not to interfere or else he'll destroy him. So Pluto fights against him instead ofAstro Boyand Brando is defeated after being taken to the bottom of the ocean. But Pluto is severely damaged afterwards, making Brando one of the few robots that were strong enough to even damage Pluto. Brando in the 1980s anime.

characters.name: Brianna
characters.description: BriannaJapanese NameロクサーヌOther NamesRoxanne (Japanese dub)GenderFemaleAffiliationSpace Academy researcherOccupationStudentJapanese VAYukanaEnglish VAOlivia Hack  Briannais an engineering student and prospective astronaut. She is a very intelligent student, and was Astro's first romantic crush. She only appears in episode 36, "Space Academy", in theAstro Boy 2003 series. Brianna is a highly skilled and intelligent student at the space academy. She can come off as an airhead, but that is only because she is almost always thinking hard about her studies. She aspires to one day be an astronaut and engineer. In the original Japanese version, Brianna finds Astro adorable and treats him like a little brother, but still maintains a polite and formal tone with him. However, in the English dub, she is strangely changed to be more coy, with some of her lines altered to seem like she is flirting with Astro.

characters.name: Bruno
characters.description: BrunoEnglish NameBrunoRaceRobotAffiliationDr. BoyntonResidenceAstro's rib cageStatusDeceased Brunois a robot creature exclusive to the1987 NOW! Comics series. He is a robot spider designed byDr. Boyntonfor the use of internally controlling Astro. Bruno has limited AI, which gives him just enough consciousness to identify threats, receive orders, find his way around, and to bite at Astro's circuitry. With a special remote control, Bruno has two command options that designate how hard he bites. Each bite is supercharged with a strong blast of electricity, enough to disorient or potentially knock out Astro. Bruno's power supply is foremost leeched from Astro's battery.

characters.name: Buddy
characters.description: BuddyJapanese NameフレンドさんEnglish NameBuddyGenderMaleRaceRobotJapanese VAMasami KikuchiEnglish VAWally Wingert  Buddywas a service robot who accompaniedAcetylene Lampwhen he was shipwrecked. Buddy was programmed to protect humans, and would provide Lamp with food and companionship. As an older model of robot, Buddy had limited AI. He attempted to learn from Lamp and other humans he had known, at one point trying to tell Lamp a joke. He would send SOS messages through a homemade device, and would bring Lamp fresh fish and fruit on a daily basis. Buddy also maintained a shelter out of the sun for Lamp. Buddy also provided moral support and encouragement that a rescue would come soon. Lamp and Buddy became friends, with Lamp so interested in Buddy's ability to emote like a human that Lamp swore it would make a scientific breakthrough if they could get off the island. For Lamp's birthday, Buddy decided to obtain ripe papaya from the top of the island. A volcano unexpectedly erupted at the top of the island's peak, stranding Buddy there. Lamp was rescued just in time by a search crew, but Buddy was left on the island and assumed for dead. This left Lamp with a lot of anguish and regret. Unbeknownst to Lamp, Buddy is now living in Metro City, with a young archaeologist who found him on the island. Buddy remembers having a human friend who called him Buddy, but can no longer remember Lamp's name or who he was.

characters.name: Bugsy
characters.description: BugsyGenderMaleAffiliationRobot Rights ForceOccupationFormer circus performer  Bugsyis a minuscule robot beetle exclusive to theNOW! Comics series.He is a former attraction inMr. Cacciatore's Robo-Circus and one of Astro's first allies. Despite his size, Bugsy is over-confident and cocky, and often eager for a fist fight. He speaks with a thick Brooklyn accent and wears a sailor hat, designed to look and act like a 1930s cartoon character. He is fond of Astro and volunteers to fightBrunofor him. He often brags about his past fights in the ring, claiming to have fought fighters named The Silicon Kid and The Mighty Micro-Dot. Because of his time in the robot circus, Bugsy severely distrusts humans. In issue #9, after Bugsy joins a group of abandoned robots who intend to go into town and attack humans. Astro intervenes, and Bugsy holds his gang back only out of respect for Astro's hopes for peace. Fortunately, Bugsy begins to tolerate humans after the robot rights bill is passed.
characters.personality_section: Despite his size, Bugsy is over-confident and cocky, and often eager for a fist fight. He speaks with a thick Brooklyn accent and wears a sailor hat, designed to look and act like a 1930s cartoon character. He is fond of Astro and volunteers to fightBrunofor him. Astro intervenes, and Bugsy holds his gang back only out of respect for Astro's hopes for peace. Fortunately, Bugsy begins to tolerate humans after the robot rights bill is passed.

characters.name: Count Burg
characters.description: Count BurgJapanese Nameブルグ伯爵GenderMaleRaceHumanFamilyMaria(wife)OccupationBusiness tycoonGangster (self-admitted)  Count Burgis a wealthy European real estate tycoon. He is the world's first human to marry a robot, but his hatred of robots and general cruelty inspires theBlue Knightto start an uprising. Burg primarily appears in theAstro Boymanga series. His surname means "castle" in German. While Count Burg puts on a calm, classy demeanour, it is all a cover for his cruel personality. Dr. Ross and his children first meet the "gentle" Burg who comes to ask forMaria's hand in marriage. He is charismatic enough to convince the whole family that he is a good man, to the point where Dr. Ross is in disbelief when Maria tells him about Burg's true nature. In reality, Count Burg is a cruel, ruthless braggart. He has immense disposable wealth and military ties. When describing his resources, Burg claims to own "eight castles, nine mountains, and three resorts"; in Japanese, this combination of numbers can be read as "ya + ku + za" (八 + 九 + 三), as inyakuza, the infamous Japanese crime syndicates. Burg will hurt as many people and robots as it takes to get his way. It is unclear if Burg genuinely loves Maria. He finds her beautiful and recognizes her as his wife, even after Maria has been amalgamated into Blue Bon's body. However, this does not stop him from killing the siblings with Blue Knight's own spear.
